other_observers: Rachel Hopper, Tony Leukering, Bill Maynard, SeEtta Moss, Brandon Percival, Mark Peterson, Andrew Spencer; Rich Miller
specific_location: 1/4 mile east of parking lot on Seel's Road along the Canon City Riverwalk
description: Medium-sized warbler with the following characteristics: black upperparts except for a small white crescent on the lower edge of the eye and a very large white wing patch (greater and median coverts); throat and upperchest black extending along the flanks below the wings, otherwise dull red belly and lower chest patch fading to mottled black and white at the crissum; the long tail showed black central feathers with at least the outer 3 rectrices entirely or near-entirely white.Behaviors: The redstart actively foraged in the mid-canopy of several cottonwood trees for the most part.Call: Called several times, each time a long, hollow "chuw".Plumage: Adult
